This endpoint returns the current driving times for each driver in the account, even if the driver is not currently associated to a vehicle.
Due to different driving times legislation, the returned objects have two structures: Europe and UnitedStates.
If the account has ELD, the API will fill in the structure UnitedStates. Otherwise, driving times will be available on the structure Europe.

Returns (units):

``` json [{ "driverName" string: Driver name, "licensePlate" string: Vehicle's license plate, "europe": { "driverId" int: Driver id, "maxContinuous" long: Maximum continuous driving (seconds), "maxDaily" long: Maximum daily driving time (seconds), "maxWeekly" long: Maximum weekly driving time (seconds), "maxBiweekly" long: Maximum biweekly driving time (seconds), "elapsedContinuous" long: Continuous driving time already spent (seconds), "elapsedDaily" long: Daily driving time already spent (seconds), "elapsedWeekly" long: Weekly driving time already spent (seconds), "elapsedBiweekly" long: Biweekly driving time already spent (seconds), "isMultiManning" bool: true if is multi manning, "remainingStopTime" long: Stop time still available (seconds), "extraHours" int: Extra driving This endpoint allows get jobs. ### Input: An object of job filter. The state field is required. The from, to, vehicles, drivers, allStatus and status fields are optional.
The state field must have a value between 1 and 3 inclusivÃ©, or 'active', 'pending' and 'history' values respectively.

The drivers field is an array with drivers ids.

``` json { "JobId": int: Job Id to associate the Task, if 0 a new Job is created "TypeId" : int: Task type "Who": { "ClientId": int?: Client Id (CRM Client) "ContactId" int?: Contact Id (CRM Client) } "Where": { "ClientAddressId": int?: Client Address Id (CRM client) "AddressLine1": string: Address line 1 "PlaceId": int?: Place Id "Latitude": double?: place latitude "Longitude": double?: place longitude "Notes": string: Additional notes about the address } "What":{ "Reference": string: Enter a reference for the task "Content": string: Products/Service "Quantity": int?: Indicate, if you must, a quantity related to the product or service "UnitId": int?: Kilogram = 1, Liter = 2, Pallet = 3 EUR-Pallet = 4 Box = 5 Container = 6 m2 = 7 "Notes": string: Annotations field "Duration": long? Duration }, "When": { "Start": Datetime?: If "NoTimeRestriction" this field must be null, If "TimeAndTolerance" it must be the date of arrival, if "TimeRange" it should be the start date of interval to arrive "End": Datetime?: If "NoTimeRestriction"/"TimeAndTolerance" this field should be null, if "TimeRange" it should be the end date of interval to arrive "ToleranceBefore": int?: If "TimeAndTolerance" and "Tolerance" is true then specify time in seconds, otherwise it should be null "ToleranceAfter": int?: If "TimeAndTolerance" and "Tolerance" is true then specify time in seconds, otherwise it should be null "ExclusionStart": Datetime?: If "Exclusion" is true, it should be the exclusion start date interval, otherwise it should be null "ExclusionEnd": Datetime?: If "Exclusion" is true, it should be the exclusion start date interval, otherwise it should be null }, "Forms":[ { "id": int: Form id, "version": int: Version of form, "name": string: Name of the form, "fields":[//Array with all form fields { "name": string: Name of the field, "defaultValue":string: Default field value, "value":string: New value } ] } ] } ```

Returns:

``` json { "JobAdded": { "Id": int : Job id, "Tasks": int[] : List of all tasks id's created, "IsNew": bool: If true, a new job was created } ```
Notes:
  • The "how" node is considered obsolete and it's data is not being updated even if specified in the request

post.description:
This endpoint provides the information that is the base of subsistence allowance report. Each item corresponds to up to an hour of stay inside a country, district, place, placeType or zone.

Input:

```json { "Vehicles" int[]: If the ShowInfoBy choice is 1, then it should be passed an int array with the vehicles ids we want to get the information, "Drivers" int[]: If the ShowInfoBy choice is 2, then it should be passed an int array with the drivers ids we want to get the information, "CoDrivers" int[]: If the ShowInfoBy choice is 3, then it should be passed an int array with the coDrivers ids we want to get the information, "StartDate" datetime: Start of data period. ex: "2021-01-01T00:00:00.000", "EndDate datetime": End of data period. ex: "2021-01-02T00:00:00.000", "ShowTrafficBy int": None = 0, Country = 1, District = 2, Place = 3, PlaceType = 4, Zone = 5 "ShowInfoBy" int : None = 0, Vehicles = 1, Drivers = 2, coDrivers = 3, DriverStatus = 4 } ```

Returns

```json [{ "vehicleId" int: Vehicle id. "driverId" int: Driver Id. "countryISO" string: standard code for the name of the country. "districtId" string: standard code for ther name of the district. "placeId" int: palce Id. "placeType id" int: place type id (list of placetypes -> v2/areas/types). "zoneId" int: zone id. "arriveTime" datetime: It's the arrival date or, if it is contiguous with the previous slot, it is the beginning of the hour slot (Company local time). "arriveTimeUtc" datetime: It's the arriveTime in UTC. "arriveOdoGps" double: It's the GPS odometer at the beginning of the hour slot. "arriveOdoCan" double: It's the canbus odometer at the beginning of the hour slot. "exitTime" datetime: It's the exit date or, if it is contiguous with the next slot, it is the end of the hour slot (Company local time). "exitTimeUtc" datetime: It's the exitTime in UTC. "exitOdoGps" double: It's the GPS odometer at the end of the hour slot. "exitOdoCan" double: It's the canbus odometer at the end of the hour slot. "mileageGps" double: The total gps mileage travled by the vehicle in the destination in the hour slot "mileageCan" double: The total canbus mileage traveled by the vehicle in the destination in the hour slot "stayTime": The total of seconds the vehicle stayed in the destination in the hour slot. }] ```

post.description:
Validates a file for the importation of routes schedules ### Input: Each line of the excel file must follow the next rules: The licensePlate or vehicleId, routeName or templateCode, routeStartTime, sendToWFMNAV and createJobs fields are required. The freeField1, freeField2, freeField3 and notes fields are optional.
One of the parameters vehicleId and licensePlate must be supplied. If both parameters are supplied, the endpoint will use only the vehicleId.
One of the parameters templateCode and routeName must be supplied. If both parameters are supplied, routeName will have priority over the templateCode.
The taskType, reference, products_Service, quantity and quantityTypeId are required if createJobs is true. Otherwise, these are optional fields.
The vehicleId value must be bigger than 0 to be considered filed. The licensePlate field can have up to 15 characters and must match the available licence plates available in the account.
The routeName field can have up to 50 characters and must match the available routes in the account.
The routeStartTime field must obey the following format: "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS".
The sendToWFMNAV field must be filled with 0 (false) or 1 (true).
The createJobs field must be filled with 0 (false) or 1 (true).
The taskType field can have up to 128 characters.
The reference field can have up to 200 characters.
The products_Service field can have up to 64 characters.
The quantity field must have a value greater than zero, up to a maximum of 2147483647.
The quantity type field must have a value between 1 and 8 inclusive.
The notes string field can have up to 2000 characters.
The freeField1, freeField2 and freeField3 can have up to 200 characters. ```json [ Array with route schedules { "licensePlate": string - License plate, "vehicleId": int - Vehicle Id "routeName": string - Template route name, "templateCode": string - Template route code, "routeStartTime": DateTime nullable - Route start time if exists, "sendToWFMNAV": bool - Sent route to WFM/NAV, "createJobs": bool - Create Jobs, "taskType": string - Task Type, "reference": string - Reference, "products_Service": string - Products/Service, "quantity": int - Quantity, "quantityTypeId": int - Quantity type id, "notes": string - Notes, "freeField1": string - Route free field 1, "freeField2": string - Route free field 2, "freeField3": string - Route free field 3 }, { ... } ] ``` ### Response: ```json [ Array with route schedules result row { "line": int - Line number, "licensePlate": string - License plate, "licensePlateOriginal": string - License plate original, "routeName": string - Template route name, "routeNameOriginal": string - Route name original, "routeStartTime": DateTime - Route start time, "sendToWFMNAV": bool - Send to WFM/NAV, "createJobs": bool - Create jobs, "taskType": string - Task type, "reference": string - Reference, "Products_Service": string - Products/Service, "quantity": int - Quantity, "quantityTypeId": int - Quantity type id, "notes": string - Notes, "invalidFields": [ List with all RouteShedulesErrors { "RouteShedulesErrors": enum - Route Schedules Errors available Invalid_Field_LicensePlate = 0, Invalid_Field_RouteName, Invalid_Field_RouteStartTime, RouteStartTime_In_Past, Reference_Greater_Length, Products_Service_Greater_Length, Quantity_Negative, Notes_Greater_Length, Only_Route_With_One_Step_Task, LicensePlate_NotFound, RouteName_NotFound, TaskType_NotFound, QuantityType_NotFound, Invalid_Field_SendRouteToWfmNav, Invalid_Field_CreateJobs, Vehicle_HasNoWfmTerminalAssociated RouteNameOrTemplateCode_Required, } ], "freeField1": string - Route free field 1, "freeField2": string - Route free field 2, "freeField3": string - Route free field 3 } ] ```

400.description:
Bad Request is returned when:
                 - Template Code and/or Route Name are not provided.
                 - VehicleId and/or LicensePlate  are not provided.

401.description:
Unauthorized is returned when:
                 - The authentication token is invalid.

404.description:
Not found is returned when:
                 - Template Code and/or Route Name are not found.

Conflict is returned when there is at least one invalid field:

Allows the client to specify which fields to include in the response via query parameters, 
and to filter the data by load unit ids if necessary.

**Sample request:**
            
    GET v2/trackingdata/latest
        ?fields=position,doorSensors,temperatureSensors,analogSensors,digitalSensors,tireSensors
        &loadunitids=1234,2345,3456

parameters.name: Fields
parameters.description:
An array of field names to include in the response.
Valid fields: position, doorSensors, temperatureSensors, analogSensors, digitalSensors, tireSensors.
If no fields are specified, the response will only return Position.

parameters.name: LoadUnitIds
parameters.description: Optional array of load unit IDs to filter the response by specific load units.

200.description: Returns the filtered load unit tracking data successfully.

400.description: Returns a bad request error if any fields or parameters are invalid.

500.description: Returns an internal server error if an unexpected error occurs.

post.description:
Returns vehicle tracking data for a given period (max 24h, not older than 5 years). Always includes basic data; extra fields can be requested.

Request:

``` { "vehicleId": 123456, // Required "dateFrom": "2025-06-01T00:00:00", // Optional (Default: DateTo - 1h) "dateTo": "2025-06-01T00:15:00", // Optional (Default: Now) "fields": [ // Optional extra double | GPS longitude ignition | bool | Ignition on/off speed | double | Speed in km/h ``` Other fields must be requested explicitly: ``` altitude | double | Altitude in meters direction | double | Finds the trips for a vehicle in a specified period. The trips considered are the ones that:
a) Have some part in the specified period: start at most at {dt} (date to) and end at least at {df} (date from). The start and end timestamps of the returned trips are always the real ones, even if the trip starts or ends outside the specified period. - plus -
b) The running trip, if one exists and started in the specified period. The requested period can not be longer then seven days. Ensures that when TripLimit is passed, date boundaries must align to quarter-hour intervals (00, 15, 30, 45 minutes).

get.summary: Get vehicles locations stored within given period
get.description:
The method receives two dates that define the period of data to return: df and dt. The filtering is done on the insert timestamp (when the record was stored in our database) and not the GPS timestamp of the record. If df and dt are not defined, the method will return the records stored in the last 15 minutes.
The list of vehicles is selected automatically based on the permissions of the API credentials.
There are two additional restrictions: the period cannot exceed 15 minutes and must be within the last 24 hours.If these conditions are not met, the method will return the status code 400 Bad Request.

parameters.name: df
parameters.description: Start of period (UTC). If a value for df is not provided, then it is assumed that it's value is 15 minutes before dt (End of period)

parameters.name: dt
parameters.description: End of period(UTC). If a value for df is not provided, then it is assumed that it's value is the current date/time.

get.summary: Get mileage and the driving time of a vehicle in a given period
get.description:
This endpoint will only return data as long as the End date is greater than the Start date and the time range (End date - Start date) is lower than a month.
            
If the time range between the dates is higher than two days, the response parameters effectiveDf and effectiveDt will be rounded by the hour, the effectiveDf will be rounded down and the effectiveDt will be rounded up.
            
### Input:
```json
{
 "vehicleId": int - Vehicle Id,
 "df": DateTime - Date that will be used as the start of the period in the search of the trips,
 "dt": DateTime - Date that will be used as the end of the period in the search of the trips
}
```
            
### Response:
```json
{
 âmileageGpsKmsâ: double - Total mileage of the vehicle,
 âmileageCanKmsâ: double - Total canbus mileage of the vehicle,
 "drivingTimeSeconds": int - Total driving time in seconds,
 "totalFuelUsed": double - Total fuel used in liters,
 "effectiveDf": DateTime - Date that was effectively used as the start of the period in the search of the trips,
 "effectiveDt": DateTime - Date that was effectively used as the end of the period in the search of the trips
}
```

parameters.name: vehicleId
parameters.description: Vehicle Id

parameters.name: df
parameters.description: Start of the period (UTC)

parameters.name: dt
parameters.description: End of the period (UTC)

400.description:
Bad Request is returned when:
- The Start date is greater than the End Date.
- The difference between the two dates is greater than a month.

post.description:
This endpoint will only return data as long as the End date is greater than the Start date and the time range (End date - Start date) is lower than a month.
            
### Input:

```json
 "IncludeAddresses": bool - Whether to lookup addresses and include them in the response
}
```
            
### Response:
```json
{

 âPassengerIdâ: string - PassengerRFID value,
 "Timestamp": DateTime - Date that the event was created,
 "Type": char - Identifies if is I or O from the Serial port number that the reading is done,
 "VehicleId": int - Vehicle identifier,

 "Longitude": DateTime - Longitude where the event was created,
 "Address": string - Address where the event was created,

 "PlaceName": string - Place name if identifier has value,
```
